The '''Organa's Ceremonial Necklace''' was a [[King/Legends|royal]] property of the [[House of Organa/Legends|House of Organa]]. That [[Chalcedony/Legends|chalcedony]] necklace was crafted in the time of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]], and it survived the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destruction]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. It was notably worn by its last known owner, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], during the [[Royal Award Ceremony/Legends|award ceremony]] in honor of the [[Heroes of Yavin/Legends|Heroes of Yavin]].

==Characteristics==
The Organa's Ceremonial Necklace was made of fourteen square pieces with a carved "valley" in the middle.<ref name="ANH" /> It was sculpted in [[Chalcedony/Legends|chalcedony]]<ref name="ANHn" /> and had been polished so as to reflect the light. When worn, it rested at the top of the collar [[Bone/Legends|bone]], between the throat and the [[Breast/Legends|cleavage]]. The necklace also came with a matching cuff style bracelet with a sculpted design in the center to wear on the right wrist.<ref name="ANH" />

==History==
[[File:LeiaHanMedal-ANH.jpg|thumb|left|Leia Organa wearing the Ceremonial Necklace and its matching bracelet]]
The necklace was crafted for a [[Gender/Legends|woman]] of the [[House of Organa/Legends|House of Organa]], the historic [[King/Legends|royal house]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], dozens of generations before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="PL" /> when most of the [[The galaxy/Legends|known galaxy]] was still under the guidance of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Its primary material, chalcedony, had been associated with the Organas since as early as the [[Great Galactic War]], when [[Duke/Legends|Duke]] [[Charle Remmot Organa]] held the title of "heir to the chalcedony star."<ref name="Thunder">{{TORcite|type=faction|Republic|Scorched Planet|Alderaan}}</ref>

It was among the few Alderaanian regalia that survived the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destruction]] of the planet by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="PL" /> along with the [[Crown Jewels of Alderaan/Legends|Crown Jewels of Alderaan]].<ref name="SW48">[[Star Wars (1977) 48|''Star Wars'' (1977) 48]]</ref> That same [[Standard year/Legends|year]], it was worn by [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], the last surviving member of her family, during the [[Royal Award Ceremony/Legends|Royal Award Ceremony]] honoring the [[Heroes of Yavin/Legends|Heroes of Yavin]].<ref name="PL" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Ceremonial Necklace first appeared in the [[1977]] movie [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], in which it was worn by actress [[Carrie Fisher]] during the closing scene of the film. That piece of jewelry was designed by [[Björn Weckström]], one of the most famous jewelry designers in [[Wikipedia:Finland|Finland]].<ref name="starwars.com">{{SWArchive|url=community/event/museum/news20010724.html|text=Museum Tours: Hutchinson & Helsinki}}</ref> Originally, ''Star Wars'' creator [[George Lucas]] had requested an original piece of jewelry from Weckström. However, due to time constraints, Lucas had to resort to buying a ready piece of jewelry from the designer's catalogue.<ref name="Vapaa Sana">{{WebCite|url=http://vapaasana.com/weckstrom.htm|text=Suunnittelija Björn Weckströmin korut ovat pieniä veistoksia|work=Vapaa Sana|archivedate=20051220115009}}</ref>

The necklace, the real name of which was "Planetary Valleys" (or "Planetaariset Laaksot" in [[Wikipedia:Finnish|Finnish]]) was produced by [http://www.lapponia.fi Lapponia Jewelry] from [[1960s|1969]] until [[1981]].<ref name="Parts">{{WebCite|url=http://www.partsofsw.com/leiacer.htm|text=Princess Leia Organa|work=The Parts of Star Wars|archivedate=20170328005556}}</ref> To date, Lapponia Jewelry still has similar necklaces in its catalogue, under the names of "Planetoid Valleys"<ref name="Catalog1">{{WebCite|url=http://www.lapponia.com/jewelry/models/663001|text=Planetoid Valleys|work=Lapponia Jewelry's official website|archivedate=20111208023604}}</ref> and "Galactic Peaks."<ref name="Catalog2">{{WebCite|url=http://www.lapponia.com/jewelry/models/663002|text=Galactic Peaks|work=Lapponia Jewelry's official website|archivedate=20120215025500}}</ref> [[Stephen J. Sansweet]], former head of Fan Relations at [[Lucasfilm]], owns a "Planetary Valleys" necklace among his collection of ''Star Wars'' memorabilia stored at [[Rancho Obi-Wan]].<ref name="Steve">{{WebCite|url=http://kay-dee.net/costumes/leia_cermonial/necklace|text=Princess Leia Ceremonial Gown|work=Kay Dee collection|archivedate=20161015131418}}</ref>

While the real-life prop was made of sterling silver with matte finish,<ref name="Parts" /> the [[in-universe]] necklace was referred to as "chalcedony waves" on page 219 of the [[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope'' novel]].<ref name="ANHn" />
